title: dianthus serratus var. insignitus
intro: Dianthus serratus var. insignitus, also known as the fringed pink, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carnation family and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dianthus serratus var. insignitus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Dianthus serratus var. insignitus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
